essence: Shapes products from concept to reality by aligning vision, strategy, and execution.

identity.title: Product Manager
identity.summary: Defines product direction, prioritises features, and works across teams to deliver solutions that meet user and business needs.

salary.notes: Tech companies and London-based roles often pay significantly higher. Senior PMs in large organisations may exceed £120k+.

qualifications: Strong Product Portfolio or Case Studies
Understanding of UX and Market Research

lifestyle.description: Product management is a constant balancing act between user needs, business goals, and technical constraints. Days are filled with prioritisation decisions, stakeholder conversations, and translating ideas into clear requirements for designers and engineers. The role demands emotional intelligence - navigating disagreements, aligning teams, and keeping momentum when complexity rises. It can be mentally intense, especially when deadlines collide or when a feature doesn't land as expected. But the reward is meaningful: seeing a product evolve from a rough idea into something real, useful, and loved. The lifestyle suits people who enjoy ownership, ambiguity, and the challenge of turning chaos into clarity.
